question: US x Iran meeting by March 31, 2026?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if there is a diplomatic meeting between representatives of the United States and Iran by the listed date, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

A diplomatic meeting refers to a deliberate meeting between representatives of the listed countries who are acting in an official capacity and are authorized to engage in negotiation or diplomacy regarding US-Iranian relations on behalf of their governments. Meetings conducted indirectly, for example, through designated mediators, facilitators, or interlocutors acting with the knowledge and authorization of the relevant governments, will qualify. 

Brief greetings, chance encounters, or talks otherwise not deliberately aimed at diplomacy or negotiation will not count.

The meeting must be in-person and must be publicly acknowledged by either government or reported by a consensus of credible media. Remote meetings, phone calls, or other meetings where the relevant parties are not present will not count.

The resolution sources for this market will be official information from the governments of the United States and Iran, and a consensus of credible reporting.

Brief greetings, chance encounters, or talks otherwise not deliberately aimed at diplomacy or negotiation will not count.

The meeting must be in-person and must be publicly acknowledged by either government or reported by a consensus of credible media. Remote meetings, phone calls, or other meetings where the relevant parties are not present will not count.

The resolution sources for this market will be official information from the governments of the United States and Iran, and a consensus of credible reporting.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket shows low implied probability for a US-Iran diplomatic meeting in the near term, driven by persistent Middle East tensions from Houthi Red Sea attacks, Israel-Hezbollah clashes, and Iran's nuclear advancements nearing weapons-grade uranium levels. Indirect Oman-mediated talks in August focused on sanctions and nuclear curbs but produced no direct bilateral commitment, despite reformist President Pezeshkian's calls for engagement. US officials insist on verifiable compliance before easing pressure, maintaining a pattern unbroken since pre-2018 negotiations. Key upcoming catalysts include IAEA monitoring reports in November and the US presidential election, which could recalibrate diplomatic postures amid high uncertainty.

question: UK Cabinet Minister resigns by June 30, 2026?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if any sitting Cabinet Minister of the United Kingdom as of February 4, 2026 ET, resigns from their position in the cabinet by June 30, 2026 ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No."

Only an actual resignation will qualify toward a "Yes" resolution. If the Prime Minister accepts a letter of resignation from a cabinet minister, or a cabinet minister otherwise formally resigns, this market will resolve to "Yes" immediately, even if the cabinet minister in question agrees to stay for a period of time for any reason (e.g., until a replacement is ready, etc.). If a minister submits a letter of resignation and it is refused, or they otherwise withdraw that letter, it will not qualify toward this market's resolution.

If a minister submits a letter of resignation and is subsequently fired from their position, it will still qualify toward a "Yes" resolution.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the government of the United Kingdom; however, a consensus of credible reporting will also be used.

events.title: UK Cabinet Minister resigns by...?
events.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if any sitting Cabinet Minister of the United Kingdom as of February 4, 2026 ET, resigns from their position in the cabinet by February 28, 2026 ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No."

Only an actual resignation will qualify toward a "Yes" resolution. If the Prime Minister accepts a letter of resignation from a cabinet minister, or a cabinet minister otherwise formally resigns, this market will resolve to "Yes" immediately, even if the cabinet minister in question agrees to stay for a period of time for any reason (e.g., until a replacement is ready, etc.). If a minister submits a letter of resignation and it is refused, or they otherwise withdraw that letter, it will not qualify toward this market's resolution.

If a minister submits a letter of resignation and is subsequently fired from their position, it will still qualify toward a "Yes" resolution.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the government of the United Kingdom; however, a consensus of credible reporting will also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader sentiment on UK Cabinet Minister resignations reflects the relative stability of Prime Minister Keir Starmer's new Labour government, formed after the July 2024 election landslide, with no high-profile departures since taking office. Recent pressures include backlash over the winter fuel payment cuts for pensioners and internal rows over donor scandals, but official statements emphasize team unity amid fiscal challenges. The Autumn Budget on October 30, led by Chancellor Rachel Reeves, looms as a key test, potentially sparking discord if tax hikes or spending cuts provoke dissent. Polls show Labour's lead holding firm, tempering odds of abrupt exits, though welfare reforms and PMQs clashes could shift dynamics quickly.

question: Will Strasbourg win the 2025-26 UEFA Europa Conference League?
description:
This market will resolve according to the team that wins the 2025-26 UEFA Europa Conference League final.

If at any point it becomes impossible for a listed team to win the 2025-26 UEFA Europa Conference League per the rules of the UEFA Europa Conference League (e.g., they are eliminated in the playoffs), the corresponding market will resolve to “No”.

If the 2025-26 UEFA Europa Conference League is cancelled, postponed after June 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, or there is otherwise no winner declared within that timeframe, this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source will be official information from UEFA; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Crystal Palace commands 28% trader consensus in the UEFA Europa Conference League winner market, propelled by Premier League depth, Eze's creativity, and Glasner's tactical setup amid a wide-open field after the league phase opener. Rayo Vallecano (17%) differentiates via La Liga tenacity and Vallecas fortress, while Strasbourg (15.5%) surges on Ligue 1 form and home wins against mid-table foes. AEK Athens (11.5%) banks on Greek passion and counter-speed, but Fiorentina (10.5%) holds knockout edge as two-time recent finalists. AZ Alkmaar (10.1%) and Mainz (9.5%) bring Eredivisie flair and Bundesliga structure, with Shakhtar (9.5%) leaning on resilient Eastern European know-how; fixture runs and rotation needs will sway shifting odds.

This market will resolve according to the team that wins the 2025-26 UEFA Europa Conference League final.

If at any point it becomes impossible for a listed team to win the 2025-26 UEFA Europa Conference League per the rules of the UEFA Europa Conference League (e.g., they are eliminated in the playoffs), the corresponding market will resolve to “No”.

If the 2025-26 UEFA Europa Conference League is cancelled, postponed after June 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, or there is otherwise no winner declared within that timeframe, this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source will be official information from UEFA; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Crystal Palace commands 28% trader consensus in the UEFA Europa Conference League winner market, propelled by Premier League depth, Eze's creativity, and Glasner's tactical setup amid a wide-open field after the league phase opener. Rayo Vallecano (17%) differentiates via La Liga tenacity and Vallecas fortress, while Strasbourg (15.5%) surges on Ligue 1 form and home wins against mid-table foes. AEK Athens (11.5%) banks on Greek passion and counter-speed, but Fiorentina (10.5%) holds knockout edge as two-time recent finalists. AZ Alkmaar (10.1%) and Mainz (9.5%) bring Eredivisie flair and Bundesliga structure, with Shakhtar (9.5%) leaning on resilient Eastern European know-how; fixture runs and rotation needs will sway shifting odds.

question: Will global temperature increase by less than 1.10ºC in March 2026?
description:
This market will resolve according to the value reported by the Global Land-Ocean Temperature Index for March 2026 when it is released.

An anomaly within a named bracket for March 2026 is necessary and sufficient to resolve this market immediately once the data becomes available, regardless of whether the figure for March 2026 is later revised.

The primary resolution source for this market will be the figure found in the table titled "GLOBAL Land-Ocean Temperature Index in 0.01 degrees Celsius" under the column "Mar" in the row "2026" (https://data.giss.nasa.gov/gistemp/tabledata_v4/GLB.Ts+dSST.txt).

If NASA’s “Global Temperature Index” is rendered permanently unavailable, other information from NASA may be used.
If no information for February 2026 is provided by NASA by May 1, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to the lowest range bracket.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader sentiment clusters around 1.20–1.29ºC for March 2026 global surface air temperature anomaly, driven primarily by the relentless anthropogenic warming trend—now embedding ~1.2ºC baseline elevation—tempered by an emerging La Niña expected to suppress temperatures through early 2026. Copernicus ERA5 data shows recent March peaks at 1.30ºC in 2024 amid El Niño, but NOAA forecasts indicate 70% La Niña odds persisting into boreal spring, widening model spreads in ECMWF and UKMO outlooks. Differentiating the top bins: subtle ENSO strength variations and fading stratospheric aerosol cooling from Hunga Tonga, with >1.29ºC hinging on neutral-to-weak La Niña or positive Indian Ocean Dipole, per WMO probabilistic guidance.

question: Spread: Colombia (-1.5)
description:
In the upcoming FIFA International Friendlies game, scheduled for March 26 at 7:30 PM ET:

This market will resolve to "Colombia" if Colombia win the game by 2 or more goals.

Otherwise, this market will resolve to "Croatia".

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50–50.

This market will resolve according to the official final score published on fifa.com. This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

events.title: Colombia vs. Croatia - More Markets
events.description: More markets for the FIFA International Friendlies game, scheduled for March 26 at 7:30 PM ET.

eventMetadata.context_description: Croatia enters as trader favorite against Colombia in this international friendly, with market odds reflecting their superior FIFA ranking (10th vs. 19th) and unbeaten run in five recent matches, including Nations League wins. Colombia's attack, led by Luis Díaz and Jhon Córdoba, poses upset potential after topping CONMEBOL qualifiers, but defensive lapses in losses to Ecuador and Paraguay temper optimism. Key watch: Croatia's Luka Modrić availability post-club duties and Colombia's altitude acclimation if hosted neutrally. Rest advantage favors rested Croats ahead of Nations League; historical head-to-head absent, but Croatia's 70% win rate vs. South American sides bolsters consensus implying 55-60% victory probability. Momentum shifts could come from late lineups.

description:
In the upcoming FIFA International Friendlies game between Colombia and Croatia, scheduled for March 26 at 7:30 PM ET:

question: Will George Pickens play for Arizona Cardinals in 2026-27?
description:
This market will resolve to the next team George Pickens officially joins by August 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET.

If George Pickens does not officially join a new team by August 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to “Other”.

If George Pickens joins a team that is not listed, this market will resolve to “Other”.

If George Pickens is released, retires, or is not under contract with any professional team by August 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to “Other”.

An official signing announcement prior to the market’s close date will immediately resolve this market to the corresponding option.

The primary resolution sources for this market will be official announcements from the NFL and/or the acquiring team. A consensus of credible media reporting may also be used to resolve the market.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus heavily favors the Dallas Cowboys (80.5%) as George Pickens' 2026-27 landing spot, driven by his contract expiring after the Steelers' fifth-year option for 2025 at $10.1 million, positioning him for unrestricted free agency amid Pittsburgh's crowded wide receiver depth chart featuring Allen Robinson II and rookie Roman Wilson. Pickens' elite downfield production—leading the NFL in yards per catch last season—pairs with recent off-field frustrations, including a sideline spat with coach Mike Tomlin and a one-game suspension threat, fueling trade speculation despite no confirmed deals. Miami Dolphins (13.5%) and Kansas City Chiefs (11.8%) trail due to quarterback needs for speedsters like Pickens alongside Tyreek Hill or next to aging Hollywood Brown, while Steelers retention odds sit low at 5% amid extension impasse reports from beat writers.

Miami Dolphins (13.5%) and Kansas City Chiefs (11.8%) trail due to quarterback needs for speedsters like Pickens alongside Tyreek Hill or next to aging Hollywood Brown, while Steelers retention odds sit low at 5% amid extension impasse reports from beat writers.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus heavily favors the Dallas Cowboys (80.5%) as George Pickens' 2026-27 landing spot, driven by his contract expiring after the Steelers' fifth-year option for 2025 at $10.1 million, positioning him for unrestricted free agency amid Pittsburgh's crowded wide receiver depth chart featuring Allen Robinson II and rookie Roman Wilson. Pickens' elite downfield production—leading the NFL in yards per catch last season—pairs with recent off-field frustrations, including a sideline spat with coach Mike Tomlin and a one-game suspension threat, fueling trade speculation despite no confirmed deals. Miami Dolphins (13.5%) and Kansas City Chiefs (11.8%) trail due to quarterback needs for speedsters like Pickens alongside Tyreek Hill or next to aging Hollywood Brown, while Steelers retention odds sit low at 5% amid extension impasse reports from beat writers.

question: Will Tesla (TSLA) close above $350 end of March?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the official closing price for Tesla, Inc. (TSLA) on the final trading day of March 2026 is higher than the listed price. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

If the final trading day of the month is shortened (for example, due to a market-holiday schedule), the official closing price published for that shortened session will still be used for resolution.

If no official closing price is published for that session (for example, due to a trading halt into the close, system issue, or other disruption), the market will use the last valid on-exchange trade price of the regular session as the effective closing price.

The resolution source for this market is Yahoo Finance — specifically, the Tesla, Inc. (TSLA) "Close" prices available at https://finance.yahoo.com/quote/TSLA/history, published under "Historical Prices."

In the event of a stock split, reverse stock split, or similar corporate action affecting the listed company during the listed time frame, this market will resolve based on split-adjusted prices as displayed on Yahoo Finance.

If the final trading day of the month is shortened (for example, due to a market-holiday schedule), the official closing price published for that shortened session will still be used for resolution.

If no official closing price is published for that session (for example, due to a trading halt into the close, system issue, or other disruption), the market will use the last valid on-exchange trade price of the regular session as the effective closing price.

The resolution source for this market is Yahoo Finance — specifically, the Tesla, Inc. (TSLA) "Close" prices available at https://finance.yahoo.com/quote/TSLA/history, published under "Historical Prices."

In the event of a stock split, reverse stock split, or similar corporate action affecting the listed company during the listed time frame, this market will resolve based on split-adjusted prices as displayed on Yahoo Finance.
